_meta.description: Machine-readable SIBC structural profile consumed by analysis/generate_skeleton.py. Companion to skeleton_profile.md (human reference). Encodes SPEC v3.0 §6.1: column->role map, decomposition labels, authored reclassification target map, artifact skip rules. The ONLY authored structural inputs are reclass_target_map and parent_overrides; everything else is deterministic column logic.
_meta.expected_node_count_note: 85 emitted entities = 86 distinct (statement, code) rows in the latest period minus the Statement 2 header artifact row (empty code, level -1). The .md's '86' counts that artifact.

period_selection.comment: Structure is taken from the latest period only. Select max report_date, then within it max date.

artifact_skip.comment: Skips the Statement 2 header artifact row (empty code, level -1, sector 'Industries (2.1 to 2.19)').

parent_overrides.basis: CSV leaves II (Food Credit) parentless at level 0; the RBI identity Bank Credit (I) = Food Credit (II) + Non-food Credit (III) makes II a primary child of I. Authored once, stable.
Same identity: I = II + III. III (Non-food Credit) is a primary child of I.

decomposition.comment: SPEC §6 step 3: a parent whose children span >1 partition value has alternate decompositions; each child's decomposition = its partition value (aliased below). Single-partition parents -> 'primary'. Only Industry (code 2) has alternates in SIBC.

exhaustive_decompositions.comment: Per (parent_partition, parent_code) -> whether its children sum to the parent. Where false, a residual is EXPECTED (RBI sectoral deployment covers a bank subset / sub-classifications are partial); the validator reports it as a note, not a failure. Unlisted parents default to exhaustive=true.

non_exhaustive.basis: Non-food Credit > 1+2+3+4: sectoral deployment is a partial coverage of non-food credit.
NBFCs (3.9) > HFCs (3.9.1) + PFIs (3.9.2): only two NBFC sub-classes are reported; the rest is unclassified.

reclass_target_map.comment: Authored. PSL memo rows (is_priority_sector_memo=True, codes i-x, Statement 1) re-slice credit already counted in the primary tree under different thresholds. additive:false. target=null where PSL-native (no single primary-tree node).

i.label: Agriculture
i.basis: PSL agriculture uses eligibility caps; primary tree is all agriculture credit.

ii.label: Micro and Small Enterprises
ii.basis: PSL MSME uses turnover-based (June 2020) definition; Industry-by-size uses a different size basis - different populations.

iii.label: Medium Enterprises
iii.basis: Same MSME definitional mismatch as ii.

iv.label: Housing
iv.basis: PSL housing applies loan-ceiling thresholds (metro/non-metro); primary tree is all housing credit.

v.label: Educational Loans
v.basis: PSL education applies an amount cap.

vi.label: Renewable Energy
vi.basis: PSL renewable maps to a subset of by-type infrastructure/power; not a clean 1:1.

vii.label: Social Infrastructure
vii.basis: PSL-native classification; no single primary-tree node.

viii.label: Export Credit
viii.basis: PSL-native; export credit is not a separate primary-tree node.

x.label: Weaker Sections
x.basis: PSL-native cross-cutting classification (incl. net PSLC- SF/MF).

signal_attach.comment: Deterministic signal_ids attachment. SIBC L1 signals carry compute.code + compute.statement = the entity identity key. Scan signals (method csv_*_scan_*) cover a family; attach to the parent entity matching compute.code/compute.statement.

concept.comment: COMPOSITION_SPEC.md §4 concept_tags. SIBC is a stock world, system-total, national — so measure/lender/geography are uniform defaults. product+segment are authored per (statement::code); sub-leaves inherit from the nearest tagged ancestor. Keys are 'statement::code' because code collides across statements (e.g. Statement 1::2.1 Micro&Small vs Statement 2::2.1 Mining).
